title: Travel agents
description: Represent and promote artists, performers, and athletes in dealings with current or prospective employers. May handle contract negotiation and other business matters for clients. Prepare cost estimates for product manufacturing, construction projects, or services to aid management in bidding on or determining price of product or service. May specialize according to particular service performed or type of product manufactured. Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. (Based on 17 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Contact motel, hotel, resort, and travel operators to obtain current advertising literature.
Confer with customers to determine their service requirements and travel preferences, and provide assistance with travel documents.
Plan routes, itineraries, and accommodation details, and compute fares and fees using schedules, rate books, and computers.
Answer inquiries regarding information such as schedules, accommodations, procedures, or policies, and inform clients of essential travel information.
Assemble and issue required documentation such as tickets, travel insurance policies, or itineraries for clients.
Maintain computerized inventories of available passenger space and provide information on available reservations.
Make and confirm reservations for transportation and accommodations using telephones, faxes, mail, and computers.
Collect payment for transportation and accommodations from customers, and prepare customer invoices.
Provide customers with travel suggestions and relevant information sources, such as guides, brochures, or maps.
Provide boarding or disembarking assistance to passengers needing special assistance.
Negotiate with clients and travel carriers regarding contractual rights and obligations, ensuring successful reservations.
Keep informed of industry trends and travel deals to provide clients with the best options.
Arrange for commercial taping sessions and accompany clients to sessions for promotional purposes.
Prepare and maintain a directory of suppliers, contractors, and subcontractors relevant for travel services.
Identify new advertising markets and propose products or services to serve them in the travel industry.
Prepare estimates of the costs of advertising products or services related to travel options for clients.
Develop reports or presentations to communicate the effectiveness of sustainability initiatives.
Promote products in online communities through weblog or discussion-forum postings, e-mail marketing programs, or online advertising.
Fill customer orders by packaging sold items and documentation for direct shipping or by transferring orders to manufacturers or third-party distributors.
Create, manage, or automate orders or invoices, using order management or invoicing software.
Determine and set product prices.
Compose descriptions of merchandise for posting to online storefront, auction sites, or other shopping Web sites.
Calculate revenue, sales, and expenses, using financial accounting or spreadsheet software.
Monitor sales staff performance to ensure that goals are met.
Develop or revise business plans for online business, emphasizing factors such as product line, pricing, inventory, or marketing strategy.
Negotiate with project stakeholders or suppliers to obtain resources or materials.
